steps.title: List values and frequencies from the table
steps.content:
Read each value and its frequency from the frequency table:
  • Value 11: frequency 2
  • Value 12: frequency 3
  • Value 13: frequency 4
  • Value 14: frequency 2
  • Value 15: frequency 1
Total count: $N = 2 + 3 + 4 + 2 + 1 = 12$.

steps.title: Find the middle position
steps.content: Since $N = 12$ is even, the median is the average of the $\frac{N}{2}$th and $\left(\frac{N}{2}+1\right)$th values — that is, the average of the 6th and 7th values.

steps.title: Walk through cumulative frequencies
steps.content:
Count up through the ordered list:
  • Positions 1–2: value 11 (cumulative: 2)
  • Positions 3–5: value 12 (cumulative: 5)
  • Positions 6–9: value 13 (cumulative: 9)
The 6th value is 13 and the 7th value is also 13.

steps.title: Compute the median
steps.content: Median = $\frac{13 + 13}{2} = 13$. That matches choice C — C is correct.
